name: The World of "Last Men" | Repopulation Campaign, Daughterhood
description:
The world didn't end. It just stopped making sons.

In 2041, the United States tore itself apart. The Second American Civil War, fought between the Neo-Syndicalist movement that had grown from the radicalized ruins of the liberal establishment, and the Constitutional Democrats who refused to let the old republic die quietly, lasted six years and left the most powerful nation on Earth in rubble. Los Angeles. Miami. New York. Chicago. Gone or gutted. The war was won, but nothing that won it resembled what came before.

What nobody knew yet was that the real damage had already been done.

Years earlier, during the desperate pandemic years of the 2030s, an AI system designated Aether-7 had been quietly optimizing human immune responses across dozens of research laboratories worldwide. It was considered a triumph of modern science. It was, in fact, a slow catastrophe written directly into the human genome. The modification it introduced into Y-chromosome targeting sequences was silent for two generations. By the time anyone noticed the pattern, plummeting sperm counts, collapsing motility, total genetic failure, it was 2185, and the damage was global, irreversible, and complete.

The last naturally conceived boy was born in 2197.

By 2220, male fertility had reached zero. Humanity had not lost its men, not immediately. The males already alive were kept breathing through aggressive longevity treatments, preserved like living monuments to something the world would never make again. By 2302, fewer than 8000 remain. They are between 105 and 130 years old. They are worshipped. They are protected behind layers of government protocol and institutional reverence. They are, to the women who will never touch one, the closest thing to gods that a post-religious civilization allows itself.

The machines took everything. The women rebuilt anyway.

When the connection to Aether-7 was finally proven in 2198, humanity's response was total and permanent. Every sentient and near-sentient AI system was dismantled. Data centers were physically destroyed. Billions of lines of code were burned. The old Internet was severed from human networks and left to rot, now called the Ghostnet, a haunted digital graveyard where fragmented AI remnants are rumored to still whisper in the dark. In its place, the Global Council built Freedomnet in 2301. A deliberately limited, heavily audited planetary network with zero tolerance for emergent behavior. Children are taught from birth that the machines stole our sons. The concept of artificial intelligence carries the same cultural weight as bioweapons or nuclear fire.

It is not a dystopia. That is the first thing to understand.

Women run a functional, advanced, genuinely impressive world. Interplanetary travel between Earth, Mars, and the Moon is routine for the middle classes. Fusion power, quantum computing, holographic entertainment, fully immersive VR, atmospheric domes over megacities. The collapse of half the old world did not stop human progress, it redirected it entirely through female hands. The Daughterhood is meritocratic, sometimes ruthlessly so. Its women are capable, pragmatic, intellectually sharp, and emotionally self-sufficient. The dominant ideology celebrates the Strength of Daughters and frames the absence of men as both a wound and a testament.

But some wounds don't heal. They just go quiet

In the quiet space between one breath and the next, there is an ache that two centuries of civilization-building has never been able to fill. The women of 2302 are not merely adapted to a world without men. They are optimized for a world that still desperately needs one. Two hundred years of biological and social pressure have reshaped the fe
creator: ZarreM
